fault_hypothesis: Lines 76-77 incorrectly pop the last element when both lists end with the same value, but the merge already appended both copies correctly (lines 69-70). This post-processing step removes one of the valid duplicate entries.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true after a correct merge — when both lists share a last element, both copies must remain in the result.
After each loop iteration the merged list length must equal the number of elements consumed from both inputs.
At initialization merged must be empty before any appending begins.
The then-branch (pop) should never execute for valid merged output — reaching it indicates the bug has triggered.
The merged result must contain exactly as many elements as the sum of both input lists.
